title: Fix legacy dependency resolution in `slice-graph.js`
objective: Make `npx create-quiver plan` exit 0 on the Quiver repo by ignoring bare spec-name values in the legacy `"dependencies"` field instead of crashing with `MISSING_DEPENDENCY`.
description: Old slice.json files (v01â€“v16) declare cross-spec dependencies with bare spec names in a `"dependencies"` array, e.g. `["quiver-v01"]`. The `normalizeDependencyRef` function in `slice-graph.js` expands bare values by prepending the current spec slug, producing refs like `quiver-v02-bootstrap-hardening/quiver-v01`. These refs do not match any slice key, causing `buildGraph` to throw `SliceGraphError: Missing dependency reference(s)`. The fix is one guard: if a bare value does not start with `slice-`, it is a spec-level legacy ref and must be returned as null (filtered). The new `"depends_on"` format with full `spec/slice-id` paths is unaffected.
must: Add a guard in `normalizeDependencyRef` that returns `null` when the bare dependency value does not start with `slice-`
Leave all other logic in `normalizeDependencyRef` and `buildGraph` unchanged
Add a unit test that exercises the legacy `"dependencies": ["quiver-vNN"]` format and confirms no error is thrown and no edge is produced
Run `node --test tests/` and confirm all tests pass
Run `npx create-quiver plan` on the Quiver repo and confirm exit 0
not_included: Changing the output or behavior of `plan`, `graph`, or `next`
Migrating old slice.json files to the new `depends_on` format
Adding warnings or notices for legacy deps â€” silent drop is intentional
acceptance: `npx create-quiver plan` exits 0 on the Quiver repo
`npx create-quiver plan --json` returns valid JSON with `plan`, `critical_path`, and `total_hours`
A test with `"dependencies": ["quiver-v01"]` does not throw and produces zero edges for that dep
A test with `"depends_on": ["other-spec/slice-01-foo"]` still produces the expected edge (regression)
tests: node bin/create-quiver.js plan --json | node -e "const d=JSON.parse(require('fs').readFileSync('/dev/stdin','utf8')); if(!Array.isArray(d.plan)) process.exit(1)"
assumptions: All old slices using bare spec names in `"dependencies"` are already completed â€” silently dropping them causes no operational loss
No slice uses a bare dep that starts with `slice-` and refers to a different spec (would be an ambiguous format)
